compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
DONE — S1 implemented in src/rebar/grounding/ (commit 352d0070).

WHAT SHIPPED (all 4 ACs met):
- AC1 evidence model: evidence.py — CLOSED reason enum (version_skew/invalid_detector/other first-class),
  refuted/match/abstain in ONE shape, coverage record; SARIF map BOTH directions (sarif.py); canonical
  grounding.schema.json is the source of truth (validated in tests).
- AC2 fail-open harness: harness.py — OUT-OF-PROCESS subprocess reaper (mirrors acli_subprocess: process-group
  SIGTERM→grace→SIGKILL→bounded drain, ESRCH/EPERM-guarded, pipe-holding-grandchild reaped) AND IN-PROCESS
  worker-subprocess (survives a hung worker AND a hard signal-death standing in for a segfaulting C-extension).
  Missing tool / timeout / crash / version-skew / spawn-OSError → recorded abstain, never raises.
- AC3 optional extra: [grounding] (tree-sitter) in pyproject + _optional registration; core import-clean
  (tree_sitter in the optionality _HEAVY gate; `import rebar.grounding` asserted leak-free).
- AC4 unit-tested: tests/unit/grounding/ — each failure mode → correct abstain(reason)+coverage. 42 grounding
  tests + the optionality + schema-coverage guards pass; mypy clean; all files < 800 LOC (largest 414).

CODE REVIEW (per epic policy, a subagent reviewed all S1 work before close):
- Found + FIXED: BLOCKER B1 (large worker result deadlocked the pipe → was misreported as timeout; now drained
  concurrently via poll/recv), MAJOR M1 (spawn OSError could raise → now fail-open abstain), MAJOR M2 (per-call
  Process FD leak → proc.close() in finally), MAJOR M3 (worker version-skew parity with run_tool added), plus
  minors (run_tool communicate OSError guard; untrusted-SARIF-bag trust flag; parse_error detail wording).
- Regression tests added for B1/M1/M3 + the untrusted-bag path.
- Accepted-as-is: a narrow-except nit in _reap_worker and the OOM-vs-segfault detail wording (both fail-open).

CONSUMERS UNBLOCKED: S2 (1532 Engine A), S3 (2554 T0 deps), S4 (48d7 Engine B) depend_on this and are now ready.

compiled_state.description:
## Why
This is the dependency root of the oracle. The three-valued evidence contract, the fail-open execution harness,
and the optional-extra scaffolding are shared by every engine — they must exist and be stable before Engine A,
T0, or Engine B are built. Getting the contract right here is what makes "one implementation, not two" real.

## What
- The normalized **evidence model**: `resolved | abstained(reason)` with a CLOSED structured reason enum
  (`unsupported_lang | no_tool | parse_error | timeout | ambiguous | private_or_internal_suspected |
  network_error | rate_limited | version_skew | invalid_detector | other`), provenance/tier, and a coverage
  record. `version_skew` (the #1 real failure) and `invalid_detector` (a project detector failing validation)
  are first-class; `other` is the explicit catch-all (no open `…`). Resolution-lane resolved value is `refuted`
  (never an asserted absence). One internal finding shape; SARIF 2.1.0 map at the edges (superset).
- The **fail-open recover/timeout harness** every backend runs inside. OUT-OF-PROCESS tools (ctags, ast-grep,
  OpenGrep, scc/lizard, registry HTTP) use the subprocess reaper (mirror `acli_subprocess`: process-group,
  SIGTERM→SIGKILL, structured timeout). IN-PROCESS bindings (tree-sitter) CANNOT be bounded by a thread/signal
  timeout (it can't interrupt a hung C-extension call, and a segfault would kill the host) — so prefer the CLI
  form of every matcher, and run any unavoidable in-process binding inside a WORKER SUBPROCESS so the same reaper
  applies. A crash/timeout/missing-tool/version-skew becomes a recorded `abstain`, never raises; the visible skip
  IS the coverage record — never a silent no-op. Each backend's version is pinned/recorded.
- **Optional-extra scaffolding**: a new extra in `pyproject.toml`, `_optional.py` guard + `guard_import`, core
  stays import-clean (the existing optionality CI gate), non-adopting clients import nothing.

## Scope
- `src/rebar/grounding/` (new package) — contract + harness + extra wiring only; no backend logic.
- Polyglot/optional by construction; no Python-specific assumptions in the contract.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Evidence model + CLOSED structured reason enum (incl. version_skew, invalid_detector, other) + coverage implemented; `refute`/`abstain` representable; SARIF map both directions.
- [ ] Fail-open harness covers OUT-OF-PROCESS (subprocess reaper) AND IN-PROCESS (worker-subprocess) backends: crash / hang / timeout / missing tool / version-skew → recorded `abstain`, never raises — incl. a segfaulting C-extension parse.
- [ ] Optional extra added; core import-clean (optionality CI gate passes); non-adopting client imports nothing.
- [ ] Unit-tested: harness converts each failure mode to the correct `abstain(reason)` + coverage entry.

file_impact.reason: evidence contract: closed reason enum, refute/match/abstain builders, normalize
fail-open harness: subprocess reaper + worker-subprocess boundary

compiled_state.title: Evidence contract + fail-open execution harness + optional-extra scaffolding (dependency root)

verify_commands.dd_text: Contract + harness + SARIF unit tests pass
